calendar.matters:
Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion; Motion For Relief From Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Dayna Ellsworth
Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Deborah T. Bjonerud, Esq.,JEANETTE N. LITTLE & ASSOCIATES, Employees of the Law Department
State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company, (925) 225-6838, deborah.bjonerud@statefarm.com, for Defendant KIMBERLY KRUMMEL
No other appearances

Matter is not contested/argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTHA CHICAS' Motion For Relief From Dismissal is denied.

Plaintiff's motion is untimely. (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. sec. 437(b) ["Application for this relief shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." (emphasis added)].) Plaintiff's counsel filed a request for dismissal of the "entire action of all parties and all causes of action" on August 4, 2021 and dismissal was entered the same day. (Ellsworth Decl., Ex. 3.) Plaintiff's counsel did not bring this motion until May 6, 2022, over nine months later. As in Younessi v. Woolf, "[t]he moving papers do not provide a reason for the delay in seeking relief." ((2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1145; see also Kendall v. Barker (1988) 197 Cal.App.3d 619, 625 ["moving party must show diligence in making the motion after discovery of the default."].) This issue is dispositive, and Plaintiff's motion is denied. (Austin v. Los Angeles Unified Sch. Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 928 ["A party seeking relief under section 473(b) must file the motion within a reasonable time but not longer than six months after the judgment or dismissal has been entered. This six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed." (emphasis added)].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)
